query_normalized: measuring epistemic resilience of llms under misleading medical context linguistics
rationale: The paper studies epistemic resilience (resistance to misleading peers/sycophancy) and trust modeling in LLMs, which aligns with the same underlying mechanism of measuring how LLMs handle misleading information (category (a) same‑mechanism evidence, also fitting the cross‑vocabulary cluster of “sycophancy/trust”). Although the domain is multi‑agent systems rather than medical contexts, it provides relevant foundational work on assessing and improving LLM epistemic robustness.
